report_name: Philippine Government Websites Hacked, Prompting Nationwide Cybersecurity Review

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Hacktivist Group
threat_actors.description: An unnamed hacktivist group that claimed responsibility for defacing the Philippine Senate website, citing 'public accountability' as a motivation. The group is also suspected in the defacement of the House of Representatives website.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Attackers likely exploited a vulnerability in the public-facing websites' content management systems (CMS), such as SQL Injection or Cross-Site Scripting, to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Server Software Component: Web Shell
attack_patterns.description: After gaining initial access, the attackers likely uploaded a web shell to the compromised server to maintain persistent access and modify website files for defacement.

attack_patterns.name: Defacement
attack_patterns.description: The attackers replaced the content of the Philippine Senate and House of Representatives websites with their own message to cause reputational damage and disrupt public information services.

malware.name: Web Shell
malware.description: A web shell was likely used by the attackers to gain persistent access to the compromised web servers and modify website content.

identities.name: Senate of the Philippines
identities.description: The official website of the Philippine Senate was defaced in a cyberattack.

identities.name: House of Representatives of the Philippines
identities.description: The official website of the Philippine House of Representatives was defaced on June 14, 2026.

identities.name: Philippine Government
identities.description: The overarching government entity whose legislative bodies were targeted by cyberattacks, prompting calls for a nationwide cybersecurity review.

identities.name: Philippine National Police (PNP)
identities.description: The national police agency of the Philippines that responded to the website defacements by calling for a government-wide review of cybersecurity measures.

indicators.name: Potential Web Shell File Creation in Web Root Directory
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Web Shell File Creation in Web Root Directory
id: 2b8a1c9d-5e6f-4a3b-8c7d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files with common web shell extensions in standard web server root directories. This is a common TTP for establishing persistence after exploiting a web application.
references:
  - https://newsinfo.inquirer.net/2246037/pnp-urges-cybersecurity-review-after-hacking-of-govt-websites
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-14
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.persistence
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.php'
      - '.aspx'
      - '.asp'
      - '.jsp'
      - '.phtml'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - 'C:\inetpub\wwwroot\'
      - 'C:\xampp\htdocs\'
  condition: all of selection_*
indicators.description: Detects the creation of files with common web shell extensions in web server root directories, a common TTP for establishing persistence after exploiting a web application.

indicators.name: Web Page Defacement via File Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: Web Page Defacement via File Modification
id: 8c7d9e0f-1a2b-4c3d-8a1b-9d5e6f2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects unauthorized modification of critical website files such as the homepage, which is a strong indicator of a website defacement attack. This rule requires a baseline of legitimate processes that are allowed to modify these files.
references:
  - https://newsinfo.inquirer.net/2246037/pnp-urges-cybersecurity-review-after-hacking-of-govt-websites
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-14
tags:
  - attack.t1491
  - attack.impact
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\index.html'
      - '\index.htm'
      - '\index.php'
      - '\default.html'
      - '\default.htm'
      - '\default.asp'
      - '\default.aspx'
  filter_legit_procs:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\deploysvc.exe'
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\explorer.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_procs
indicators.description: Detects unauthorized modification of critical website files such as the homepage, which is a strong indicator of a website defacement attack.

relationships.target_name: House of Representatives of the Philippines

relationships.source_name: House of Representatives of the Philippines
Potential Web Shell File Creation in Web Root Directory
Web Page Defacement via File Modification
